item.title: French telecoms groups raise takeover offer for Patrick Drahi’s SFR
item.description: Deal would be a key test of antitrust regulators’ views on in-market consolidation

item.title: Uber expands its stake in Delivery Hero in €270mn deal
item.description: German delivery group’s largest shareholder Prosus has agreed to reduce its stake over EU competition concerns

item.title: Energy crisis relief must support green goals, says EU climate chief
item.description: Also in this newsletter: EU lawmakers clash over bank capital rules

item.title: FirstFT: Mandelson fiasco takes down UK Foreign Office official
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Israel-Hizbollah ceasefire and German economy stagnates

item.title: Jardines and CK Hutchison pursue Hong Kong supermarket megadeal
item.description: Merger of ParknShop with Wellcome would create a dominant player in the territory’s food retail market

item.title: Europe embraces mega M&A
item.description: The EU is planning its biggest relaxation of corporate merger rules in decades

item.title: Latest AI models could threaten world banking system, financial officials warn
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude Mythos Preview model could expose vulnerabilities in lenders’ cyber defences

item.title: Wall Street banks start trading derivatives to bet on pain in private credit
item.description: JPMorgan and Barclays are among those offering CDS on Apollo, Ares and Blackstone funds

item.title: Merger rules to favour scale and innovation, says EU competition chief
item.description: Teresa Ribera confirms consumer interest will no longer be main criterion in approving or rejecting corporate deals

item.title: UK farmers face tough choices as war pushes up costs
item.description: Fertiliser and fuel prices have risen against a backdrop of depressed wheat and milk markets

item.title: Why Coutts is making an unexpected play for video games
item.description: The over 300-year-old lender is touting its status as a major banker to the sector

item.title: Recruiters hit by hiring slowdown in Europe
item.description: PageGroup chief warns Iran war has heightened uncertainty in hiring outlook for the rest of the year

item.title: OnlyFans nears deal to sell minority stake at more than $3bn valuation
item.description: Discussions with Architect Capital come less than a month after the UK-based platform’s owner Leonid Radvinsky died

item.title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars off the streets of Warsaw
item.description: Video of Rolex-wearing robot goes viral as it becomes Poland’s first robotic influencer on social media

item.title: Why we must shield our children from social media
item.description: Protecting kids from techno-oligarchs is a legal and moral duty — and other governments should follow Spain’s example

item.title: Inside China’s probe of Meta’s ‘conspiratorial’ $2bn Manus deal
item.description: The landmark tech exit has quickly become central to China’s national security agenda

item.title: Addison Lee drivers take income hit after Emirates business-class slump
item.description: Drivers face high vehicle costs on top of rising fuel prices as fares from Gulf airline drop

item.title: Japanese doctors warn Iran war threatens medical supplies
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi orders release of glove stockpile as concerns grow across Asia

item.title: US private credit firm backs loans for World Cup ticket flipping
item.description: Eagle Point Credit Management to capitalise on high prices and demand of football fans to attend the tournament

item.title: Scramble for biodiesel as price drops below regular diesel for first time
item.description: Oil squeeze driven by Iran war triggers rush to secure replacements

item.title: Data centre delays threaten to choke AI expansion
item.description: Almost 40% of such builds in US risk hold-ups, including projects tied to Microsoft and OpenAI

item.title: The white-collar defence lawyers with nothing to do
item.description: The Trump administration’s approach to justice has made life quieter for those who defend alleged financial criminals

item.title: It charges in five minutes. But can the Denza Z9GT EV crack the competition?
item.description: Rory FH Smith gets the first drive

item.title: City of London urges Swiss airports to give UK travellers e-gate access
item.description: Long queues frustrate executives and tourists entering the country, says corporation’s policy chair

item.title: Will the energy shock change global trade imbalances? With Brad Setser
item.description: Higher oil prices may not be enough to dent the world’s biggest trade surplus

item.title: Anthropic in talks to give US government access to its Mythos model
item.description: Discussions come despite federal lawsuits over whether the start-up is a national security risk

item.title: Former EY employee sues firm over dismissal after viral Israel speech
item.description: George Washington University graduate says she was fired after remarks criticising actions in Gaza

item.title: New York City’s super-rich cry foul over proposed tax on second homes
item.description: Billionaire hedge fund managers accuse mayor Zohran Mamdani of ‘demonising philanthropists’

item.title: Netflix founder Reed Hastings to step down from board
item.description: Chair to depart as streamer reports profit boost from $2.8bn termination fee after Warner Bros bidding war

item.title: Boing! Spring time for the markets
item.description: Investors seem delighted, but why?
